records.ety: [OE. halowen, halwien, halgien, AS. hālgian, fr. hālig holy. See Holy.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Hallowed(-ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Hallowing.]

defs: To make holy; to set apart for holy or religious use; to consecrate; to treat or keep as sacred; to reverence.
quotes: Hallow the Sabbath day, to do no work therein.
His secret altar touched with hallowed fire.
In a larger sense . . . we can not hallow this ground [Gettysburg].
